steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage describes people at a gathering trying to hew out their conception of art — using the metaphor of chopping or carving with an axe. The blank must name what they are trying to do with their artistic idea.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
Substitute each choice into the passage and test it against the metaphor of carving out an idea for others to understand:
  • Choice A — Visualize: to form a mental image — suggests picturing art privately, but the speakers are trying to communicate their conception to the group, not just imagine it. ✗
  • Choice B — Define: to state the meaning of clearly — fits the metaphor of carving out a clear articulation of one's conception of art and putting it into words for others. ✓
  • Choice C — Contract: to make smaller or enter an agreement — neither meaning fits the act of trying to express an artistic idea. ✗
  • Choice D — Emulate: to imitate or try to match — implies copying someone else's conception, but the passage says each person tried to express his own conception. ✗
That matches Choice B — Define.
